interactions.summary: The recommendation segment extends the education discussion through Chinese writing: David recommends Asia’s Orthographic Dilemma, Kaiser defends character learning, and Jiang recommends Proust and the Squid to explain reading-brain costs of non-phonological script.
Kaiser recommends Baidu Predict, describing big-data predictions for sports, travel, disease, real estate, box office, and Gaokao score dispersion, while the hosts joke about determinism.
Jiang recommends education books: Judith Rich Harris, David Brooks, Daniel Pink’s Drive, and Malcolm Gladwell’s Outliers, emphasizing motivation, creativity, and useful debate.

speaker_notes.note: Baidu Predict claims are Kaiser’s recommendation, not Jiang’s education argument.

claims.claim: Jiang says Chinese script is not phonological enough to read from characters alone, separating spoken and written language and making language learning take many more years.
claims.temporal_scope: Reading-brain claim made in the 2014-07-14 Sinica episode while recommending Marianne Wolf.

claims.claim: The hosts present Chinese character learning as both a burden on educational efficiency and a possible training in linking abstract symbols to ideas.
claims.temporal_scope: Recommendation discussion in the 2014-07-14 Sinica episode.

claims.claim: Jiang recommends Drive because motivation can enhance or destroy student creativity, making it useful for educators.
claims.temporal_scope: Book recommendation in the 2014-07-14 Sinica episode.

signature_moments.moment: Chinese literacy becomes a neural and temporal tax because speech and script are not tightly joined.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the spoken language and the written language are separate
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It adds a material reading-brain layer to the earlier test-and-memorization critique.

signature_moments.moment: The Gaokao predictor makes education feel accurate and creepy at once.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The joke shows the dark side of predictive education metrics at the end of a testing episode.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A writing system where characters do not reliably tell the reader how to pronounce them, increasing learning burden.
The neuroscience of how humans learn to read, invoked through Marianne Wolf’s Proust and the Squid.
Kaiser’s example of large-scale big-data prediction, including Gaokao score projections.

chronology_notes.note: Kaiser describes Baidu Predict features active at the time of the 2014-07-14 Sinica episode and upcoming stock/real-estate/box-office prediction features.
